analytics_features.name: Yield Forecasting
analytics_features.description: Predict end-of-season yields per field using historical performance, in-season sensor data, and localized weather forecasts.

analytics_features.name: Irrigation Recommendations
analytics_features.description: Optimize irrigation schedules based on soil moisture trends, crop stage, and evapotranspiration estimates.

analytics_features.name: Pest & Disease Alerts
analytics_features.description: Receive early warnings for pest and disease pressure using microclimate conditions and regional risk models.

analytics_features.name: Weather Alerts
analytics_features.description: Get field-level alerts for frost, heat stress, high wind, and severe storms tied to your sensor locations.

analytics_features.name: Soil Moisture Trends
analytics_features.description: Visualize soil moisture patterns over time by depth and zone to fine-tune irrigation and fertigation.

analytics_features.name: ROI & Payback Reporting
analytics_features.description: Track input savings, yield gains, and payback period for your sensor and analytics investments.

case_studies.title: Corn Grower in Iowa Increases Yields by 18% with Soil-Linked Irrigation
case_studies.summary: A 2,000-acre Iowa corn operation used networked soil moisture sensors and analytics to boost yields by 18% while cutting water use by 12%.
case_studies.content: This Iowa corn grower deployed soil moisture probes across variable soil zones and integrated real-time data into our analytics platform. By following irrigation recommendations and yield forecasting insights, they re-timed 27% of irrigations, avoided over-watering during critical growth stages, and ultimately harvested an 18% yield increase versus their 5-year farm average. The project achieved a 16‑month payback thanks to higher grain revenue and reduced pumping costs.

case_studies.title: Nebraska Pivot Corn Fields Gain 22% Yield with Sensor-Guided Irrigation
case_studies.summary: A Nebraska grower used in-field sensors and irrigation recommendations to achieve a 22% yield lift on pivot-irrigated corn with 18% less water.
case_studies.content: By installing a mix of soil moisture probes and a central weather station, the Nebraska farm shifted from calendar-based watering to soil-driven decisions. Our platform flagged zones at risk of stress and recommended shorter, more frequent runs. End-of-season analysis showed a 22% yield increase compared to adjacent non-instrumented fields, alongside 18% lower water use. The grower also saw improved standability and grain quality.

case_studies.title: Brazilian Corn-Soy Rotation Improves Corn Yield by 16% Using Pest Alerts
other_metrics: Improved spray timing on 85% of fields
case_studies.summary: On a Brazilian corn–soy rotation, microclimate-driven pest alerts helped raise corn yields by 16% while cutting insecticide passes by 30%.
case_studies.content: The operation integrated field-level weather data with regional pest models to trigger alerts for fall armyworm and other key pests. Timely scouting and targeted spraying replaced prophylactic applications. Over two seasons, corn yield rose by 16% and input costs dropped, with fewer off-target spray events. The analytics dashboard highlighted high-risk windows by field, helping the agronomy team prioritize scouting routes.

case_studies.title: French Maize Farm Combines Soil Sensors and Weather Data for 14% Yield Gain
other_metrics: Better stand uniformity across sandy parcels
case_studies.summary: A 600-hectare maize producer in France realized a 14% yield improvement by combining soil moisture probes with hyperlocal weather monitoring.
case_studies.content: With variable soils and recurring drought stress, the French operation used soil moisture trends and field-specific weather alerts to refine irrigation start and stop thresholds. Analytics highlighted underperforming zones where shallow rooting limited water uptake. Adjusted irrigation strategies and split nitrogen applications improved plant health through tasseling, leading to a 14% yield gain versus historical averages.

case_studies.title: Dryland Corn in Kansas Adds 11% Yield with Improved Planting Timing
case_studies.summary: Using field-level moisture and temperature data, a Kansas dryland corn grower improved planting windows and gained 11% in yield.
case_studies.content: By tracking seedbed conditions and short-term weather forecasts, the grower delayed planting in fields forecasted for cold, wet conditions. Emergence uniformity improved, and the farm avoided replants on 60% of historically problematic acres, generating an 11% yield lift on dryland corn.

case_studies.title: Iowa Soybeans Cut Irrigation Water Use by 27% with Moisture Trends
case_studies.summary: A central Iowa soybean producer used soil moisture trend analysis to reduce irrigation water use by 27% with stable yields.
case_studies.content: Field-by-field soil moisture thresholds were established, and irrigations were triggered only when root-zone moisture fell below agreed limits. The farm maintained yields while substantially cutting water and energy use.

case_studies.title: UK Winter Wheat Farm Saves 19% on Input Costs with Analytics
case_studies.summary: A UK winter wheat operation leveraged field variability maps and disease alerts to cut input costs by 19% while maintaining yield.
case_studies.content: Disease risk alerts informed fungicide timing and allowed the farm to skip low-risk passes. Variable-rate nitrogen recommendations reduced over-application in historically strong zones.

case_studies.title: Texas Cotton Grower Saves 33% Water with Sensor-Guided Deficit Irrigation
case_studies.summary: A West Texas cotton farm used deficit irrigation guided by soil moisture sensors to save 33% water with modest yield gains.
case_studies.content: Continuous soil moisture monitoring allowed the grower to maintain cotton within a controlled stress band. Despite less applied water, lint yields rose by 9% and quality grades improved.

case_studies.title: Australian Mixed Farm Achieves 15-Month Payback Across Corn, Wheat, and Cotton
case_studies.summary: A diversified Australian operation spanning corn, wheat, and cotton reached system payback in 15 months using bundled sensors and analytics.
case_studies.content: By coordinating irrigation and input decisions across multiple crops using a unified analytics dashboard, the farm captured yield and input efficiency gains that drove a rapid payback on hardware and software investments.

case_studies.title: South African Maize Fields Boost Yield by 20% with Zone-Specific Sensor Bundles
other_metrics: Improved stand uniformity in sandy hillsides
case_studies.summary: Zone-targeted sensor bundles and analytics helped a South African maize grower increase average yields by 20% across variable fields.
case_studies.content: The grower installed more dense sensor coverage on historically low-yielding hillsides. Analytics highlighted under-fertilized areas and timing mismatches, informing variable-rate applications and revised planting populations. The result was a 20% whole-farm yield increase with reduced fertilizer waste.

case_studies.title: Brazilian Soybean Farm Reduces Pest Losses with Microclimate Alerts
other_metrics: Lower resistance pressure on key chemistries
case_studies.summary: A Brazilian soybean operation used pest and disease alerts tied to local weather data to cut sprayed hectares by 26% and reduce yield losses.
case_studies.content: Field-specific alerts helped prioritize scouting and avoid unnecessary broad-spectrum applications. Savings in chemical and application costs contributed to higher profitability per hectare.

case_studies.title: Kazakhstan Wheat Farm Increases Yield by 17% with Weather-Aware Seeding
other_metrics: Reduced frost damage on emerging stands
case_studies.summary: By aligning seeding dates with localized frost and moisture forecasts, a Kazakhstan wheat enterprise achieved a 17% yield uplift.
case_studies.content: The farm’s weather stations provided more accurate temperature and moisture data than distant public stations. Forecast-driven seeding decisions reduced stand losses and improved grain quality.

case_studies.title: Romanian Maize Operation Achieves 25% Yield Increase with Full-Farm Analytics
case_studies.summary: A large-scale Romanian maize producer combined soil sensors, weather stations, and analytics to raise yields by 25% with a 14‑month payback.
case_studies.content: The farm deployed sensor bundles on more than 4,000 hectares and used analytics to coordinate irrigation, nitrogen timing, and pest management. High-resolution field maps and yield forecasts focused attention on underperforming zones, producing a 25% yield gain compared to the previous 3‑year average.

case_studies.title: Irrigated Corn in Mexico Gains 15% Yield with Seasonal Forecast Integration
case_studies.summary: A Mexican irrigated corn grower integrated seasonal climate forecasts with soil moisture data to secure a 15% yield increase.
case_studies.content: By monitoring seasonal drought risk and adjusting acreage and hybrid choice accordingly, the farm paired in-season irrigation adjustments with strategic planning decisions, leading to higher overall yields and lower water costs.

case_studies.title: Greenhouse Vegetables Use Microclimate Control to Boost Revenue
other_metrics: Higher packout rates due to uniform quality
case_studies.summary: Greenhouse vegetable producers used dense sensor networks to fine-tune microclimate control, improving revenue and cutting energy use.
case_studies.content: Temperature and humidity sensors fed optimization models for venting and heating. Growers achieved more consistent quality, higher packouts, and lower energy bills.

case_studies.title: Ukrainian Maize Farm Adds 19% Yield with Denser Sensor Coverage
other_metrics: Improved drainage management in low-lying areas
case_studies.summary: By increasing sensor density in variable fields, a Ukrainian maize producer recorded a 19% yield increase and input savings.
case_studies.content: The farm used soil moisture and EC sensors along with terrain mapping to identify compacted and poorly drained zones. Targeted tillage and drainage changes combined with variable fertilizer rates delivered significant yield gains.
